prompt: Generate a chest X-ray of the lung from the mask image, with a nodule in the upper lobe.
prompt_refined: Generate a photorealistic chest X-ray image. Utilize the lung segmentation mask provided in image 1 to precisely define the anatomical shape and boundaries of both lungs. The X-ray should feature a solitary, well-defined nodule located within an upper lobe of one of the lungs.
objects: Left lung (with anatomical shape and boundaries defined by image 1)
Right lung (with anatomical shape and boundaries defined by image 1)
Solitary, well-defined nodule located within an upper lobe of one of the lungs
Ribs (as typically seen in a chest X-ray)
Heart shadow (as typically seen in a chest X-ray)
Diaphragm (as typically seen in a chest X-ray)
Spinal column (as typically seen in a chest X-ray)
